attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who targets intermediate points (sprints and KOM) for visibility rather than stage wins. Attacks early in stages to maximize exposure time.
team_tactics: Israeli Academy uses him for promotional breakaways rather than protected riding. Given freedom to hunt points classifications and camera time without expectation of stage victory contention.
terrain_preference: Suited to transitional stages where breakaways can gain extended airtime. Less effective in pure sprint finishes where positioning chaos becomes critical.
vulnerability: Exposed in late-race technical sections and crashes due to positioning further back in bunch. Sprint finishes show limitations when peloton remains intact.
key_patterns: Targets intermediate points from breakaways for team visibility (2 instances observed)
Affected by late-race positioning chaos, caught behind splits/crashes (2 instances)
Deployed for promotional/exposure tactics rather than results-oriented missions (2 instances)
form_trajectory: unknown: insufficient recent data, but 2020 incident suggests continued domestique/breakaway role rather than leadership evolution
